name: Spero Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Spero Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel treatments for rare diseases and diseases with high unmet need. Its lead product candidate, tebipenem HBr, is an oral carbapenem for adult patients with complicated urinary tract infections (cUTIs), including pyelonephritis. The company does not have any approved products and has not generated revenue from product sales.
sellsTo: patients with complicated urinary tract infections

exposures.sentence: Our clinical-stage product candidate, tebipenem HBr, has completed a second Phase 3 trial, and has the potential to be the first oral carbapenem to treat adult patients with complicated urinary tract infections (cUTIs), including pyelonephritis, caused by certain microorganisms.

catalysts.event: FDA PDUFA decision on tebipenem HBr NDA
catalysts.sentence: GSK reported that the Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) date has been set as June 18, 2026.

tamClaims.claim: There are an estimated 3.4 million cases of cUTI each year in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: With an estimated 3.4 million cases each year in the United States, cUTI is a leading cause of infection-related hospitalization.

tamClaims.claim: The annual economic impact of antibiotic-resistant infections in the U.S. is at least $20 billion in excess direct health care costs plus $35 billion in lost productivity.
tamClaims.sentence: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) estimates that the annual impact of antibiotic-resistant infections on the U.S. economy is at least $20 billion annually in excess direct health care costs, with an additional $35 billion in lost productivity.
